parts.text:
I've listed the contents of the current directory. Here's what I found:

### Directories:
- `.amazonq` - Likely Amazon Q integration files
- `.claude` - Claude AI integration files
- `.git` - Git repository data
- `.github` - GitHub configuration files (likely workflows, templates, etc.)
- `.opencode` - Possibly related to OpenCode functionality
- `.work-agent` - Work agent configuration or data
- `dist` - Distribution/build output directory
- `node_modules` - Node.js dependencies
- `openspec` - Likely contains OpenAPI specifications
- `schemas` - JSON schemas or other schema definitions
- `src` - Source code
- `src-tauri` - Tauri-specific source code (Rust-based desktop app framework)
- `src-ui` - UI-specific source code

### Files:
- `.env.example` - Example environment variables file
- `.gitignore` - Git ignore configuration
- `CLAUDE.md` - Documentation related to Claude AI
- `README.md` - Project readme
- `VOLTAGENT_IMPLEMENTATION.md` - Documentation about Volt agent implementation
- `app-icon.png` - Application icon image
- `esbuild.config.mjs` - ESBuild configuration
- `package-lock.json` - NPM dependency lock file
- `package.json` - NPM package configuration
- `tsconfig.json` - TypeScript configuration
- `vite.config.ts` - Vite build tool configuration
- `vitest.config.ts` - Vitest testing framework configuration
- `vitest.setup.ts` - Vitest setup file

This appears to be a JavaScript/TypeScript project that uses Tauri (for desktop app functionality), has testing with Vitest, and includes integrations with AI services like Claude. The project structure suggests a modern web application with separate UI code and possibly a desktop application wrapper.
